City council approves new bike lane network

The city council voted 7-2 on Tuesday evening to approve a 14-mile expansion of the protected bike lane network, with construction set to begin next spring and continue in phases through 2026.

The expansion connects the riverside greenway to three neighborhoods that have repeatedly asked for safer cycling access, including the long-promised connection to the elementary schools on the east side.

Funding comes from a mix of state transportation grants and a small municipal bond approved by voters in 2023. Council member Lopez, who introduced the proposal, said the design draws on lessons from the 2021 pilot project on Maple Street.

What changes for drivers

Three avenues will lose one travel lane each, replaced with protected bike infrastructure separated by a concrete buffer. Parking on those avenues remains untouched.
